observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to use the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due
and collectible for the year as basis in setting up the Real Property Tax
(RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables at the beginning of the
year to reflect the correct balances as of December 31, 2021.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Treasurer, also the GSO-Designate, and the Municipal Accountant
to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property
records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and
adjust any discrepancy that will be noted, and create a Disposal Committee
to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid
further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income
from its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer, also the GSO-designate, and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted, and create a Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer as the GSO-Designate will conduct a full and complete reconciliation of PPE accounts, then update the PPE Ledger and Property Cards to determine correct balances. Moreover, there is a plan to undertake disposal of unserviceable properties this year.
observations.reason: The reconciliation between the municipal accounting records and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE was not completed and the unserviceable properties have not yet been disposed of.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to continue exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the four parcels of lot valued at P301,100.00 in the name of the Municipality, and to determine/identify the unidentified municipal lots worth P5,801,641.81 through the reconciliation of the accounting records and the property records as well as with the records of the Municipal Assessor to avoid possible third party claims that may arise in the future, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality identified some parcels of land covered by Deeds of Conveyance/ Sale and/or Title decreasing the unaccounted municipal lots totaling P2,539,631.81. Nine parcels of land valued at P1,976,841.00, and unidentified/unaccounted municipal lots totaling P2,539,631.81 were still not supported with TCT duly issued by Registry of Deeds as of this date. Awaiting the approval of the survey made by the Geodetic Engineer. After which, the Municipality will proceed with the titling process. The observation was first raised in the CY 2013 AAR.
observations.reason: COVID-19 pandemic, the community quarantine restrictions and weather conditions which hindered the Geodetic Engineer to conduct the survey and for the non-reconciliation of records of the Accounting Office, the General Services Office and the Assessor’s Office, thus exposing the subject lots to third party claims and may result in the overstatement of the assets of the government, thereby affecting its fair presentation in the financial statements which is not in accordance with Paragraph 27 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries to reflect the affected account/s in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph No. 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office made adjusting entries dated March 31, 2022 in the books of the General Fund and the Trust Fund to correct the recording made for the CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a) coordinate with the source agencies and deposit the unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds to the accounts of the source agencies and to the National Treasury as applicable; and b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous entry made in the books of accounts upon liquidation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has remitted P10,371.92 of P1,508,141.43 to the Bureau of Treasury per Check No. 709412 dated May 12, 2021. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjusting/correcting entry dated March 31, 2021 in the books of accounts to correct the entry made on the remittance of the balance of DSWD AICS amounting to P2,000.00 upon its liquidation in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The unexpended/unutilized balances of Trust Funds received from the National Government Agencies remain unremitted in CY 2021 since it cannot be established due to the lapse of time despite the exhaustion of efforts to retrieve the files.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to continually coordinate with the DILG and other agencies involved in CBMS as provided in the IRR of RA 11315 to expedite the completion of CBMS that will establish/create an updated GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is joining the 2022 CBMS nationwide roll out and currently preparing the requirements such as MOA and SB Authority, among others.
observations.reason: The Municipality is joining the 2022 CBMS nationwide roll out and currently preparing the requirements such as MOA and SB Authority, among others.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 and require the Municipal Engineer to submit the building plans and other documents in order to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Non-submission of necessary compliance documents by the Municipal Engineer and community quarantine restrictions which impeded the processing of the application, contrary to COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656. The application for property insurance of the concerned properties for CY 2022 is already being prepared. The observation was first raised in the Part II of CY 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, for audit and evaluation of programs, projects and activities charged to the fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted the utilization reports for the months of January to October 2021. Utilization reports for the months of November and December 2021 were not submitted to the Audit Team due to the MDRRMO being part of triage at Dinahican, Infanta, Quezon in connection with COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Workload brought about by COVID-19 pandemic which required the MDRRMO to be part of triage at Dinahican, Infanta, Quezon, resulting in non-submission of utilization reports for November and December 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to: • follow-up from the NSWMC the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan 2016-2025; • coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to achieve the Municipality’s sanitary landfill full operation; • continue to monitor the establishment and operation of MRF in the two remaining barangays; and require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Program of Works to the Municipal Budget Office so that any deficiency in the amount allocated for the construction of Material Recovery Facility will be included in the supplemental budget in accordance with implementing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: A follow-up inquiry was made to expedite the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan 2016-2025. A new MRF was constructed while the development of sanitary landfill is ongoing. The Municipal Engineer submitted the Program of Works to the Municipal Budget Office and the new MRF was constructed. All barangays have existing and operational MRF.
observations.reason: Approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan 2016-2025 is pending with NSWMC; development of sanitary landfill is still ongoing; two barangays were yet to establish MRF but a new MRF was constructed and the Municipal Engineer submitted Program of Works to the Municipal Budget Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer,
also the GSO-designate, and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and
complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to
establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that
will be noted, and create a Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate
disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and
possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor,
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to
continue exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the four
parcels of lot valued at P301,100.00 in the name of the Municipality and to
determine/identify the unidentified municipal lots worth P5,801,641.81
through the reconciliation of the accounting records and the property
records as well as with the records of the Municipal Assessor to avoid
possible third party claims that may arise in the future, and for the fair
presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required
under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary correcting/adjusting entries to reflect the transactions accurately to
achieve the fair presentation of the affected account/s in the financial statements in
accordance with Paragraph No. 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to establish the availability of
the cash pertaining to these unutilized trust funds and after which,
require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the accounts of the source
agencies the unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds or to the
National Treasury for unidentified source agencies; and
b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous entry
made on the remittance of the balance of DSWD AICS amounting to
P2,000.00 upon its liquidation in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor initiate the creation of permanent position for General Services Officer with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and create a Committee to undertake immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal. Thereafter, require the Municipal Treasurer, also the ICO-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted, and update the PPE ledger and property cards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor is considering the creation of permanent position for General Services Officer which will be discussed with the Local Finance Committee and include the item for GSO to the 2022 Annual Budget. Also, the former Municipal Engineer which will be retired in CY 2020 discussed with the Local Finance Committee for its inclusion in the CY 2022 budget. The Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer/ICO-GSO already started the reconciliation of records and the updating of PPE ledger and property cards. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Permanent position for the General Services Officer was not yet created. Also, the former Municipal Engineer retired in CY 2020 and the current Municipal Engineer assumed office in December 2020. The non-completion of the reconciliation between the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and the accounting records during CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to continue exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the four parcels of lot valued at P301,100.00 in the name of the Municipality and to determine/identify the unidentified municipal lots worth P5,801,641.81 through the reconciliation of the accounting records and the property records as well as with the records of the Municipal Assessor to avoid possible third party claims that may arise in the future, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor informed the Audit Team that the titling of the eight identified lots owned by the Municipality which was previously contracted with the Geodetic Engineer for surveying in March 2020 is now in process. This includes two out of the four parcels of lot recommended for immediate titling: 1) Cemetery Lot-Extension (from Crispin Tena) and 2) Sitio Linang-Dumpsite (from Heirs of Prudencio Necio), both with an area of 5,000 sqm. The applications for special patent to these lots were already submitted to the DENR for the release of Original Certificate of Title (OCT). Likewise, Sitio-Linang Dumpsite with an area of 7,000 sqm. is already titled in the name of the Municipality under TCT No. T-31403 but with the value of P140,000.00 based on the Deed of Sale in the possession of the Municipal Assessor. As for the remaining parcels of land, the Subdivision Plans by the DENR are now being processed for approval. After which, the title may then be transferred to the Municipality. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor admitted that despite the reconciliation between Accounting, Assessor’s and ICO-GSO records, a significant amount remains unidentified. Considering the unaccounted balance is from previous years and/or administrations and all remedies at hand were already exhausted, the management concurs that this amount includes cost of old Municipal Building and other structures.
observations.reason: The community quarantine restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic and weather conditions hindered the Geodetic Engineer to conduct the survey. Also, the preparation of technical description for untitled lots is still in process, thus exposing the subject lots to third party claims and may result in overstatement of assets. A significant amount remains unidentified despite reconciliation efforts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. the Municipal Accountant to complete and update the supplies ledger cards to support the balances of all inventory accounts of the Municipality; b. the ICO-GSO to maintain and update stock cards for the receipts and issuances of supplies to support the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and c. the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-GSO to coordinate for the reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments, if any, for the fair presentation of the inventory account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has designated personnel to prepare and update Stock Ledgers Card for Inventory. The Municipal Accountant prepared an inventory and the ICO-GSO assigned personnel to prepare and update Stock Ledgers Card for all kinds of Inventory. Periodically, the Municipal Accountant and the Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer/ICO-GSO to reconcile records and made the necessary adjustment.
observations.reason: The non-reconciliation of records between the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the accounting records in the amount of P238,250.00 as of December 31, 2020. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert utmost effort to locate the documents to support the unaccounted balance in the Other Payables account and to reconcile and settle the same in accordance with the provisions of Section 59 of PD No. 1445 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed prior records and determined that the balance is almost 10 years in existence. Thus, she reverted the amount to Government Equity.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant determined that the balance is almost 10 years in existence and could not be fully supported; hence she reverted the amount to Government Equity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest expenses and consequently adjust the computation and recording of the asset’s depreciation expense.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared an adjusting entry under JEV No. 2020-06-02060 capitalizing the recorded borrowing costs on the acquired heavy equipment in CY 2019. Likewise, the computation and recording of the asset’s depreciation expense was adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in retrieving the records/schedules of these funds for verification. Also, to confirm with the source agencies any availability of cash due them. After which, require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the source agencies the unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds or to the National Treasury for unidentified source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already remitted unexpended balances totaling P2,487.28 to the National Treasury and other source agencies. The Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer is establishing the records of the trust accounts to check the availability of the cash pertaining to the unutilized trust funds and will remit accordingly. A correcting/adjusting entry was already made to reflect the correct balance and to generate the liquidated DSWD-AICS grant.
observations.reason: Other projects with unexpended/unutilized balances totaling P1,508,141.43 remained unremitted as of December 31, 2020 since the Municipal Accountant has not established the availability of the cash pertaining to these funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and their staff, create a simple program thru MS Excel that will generate real time schedule of RPT and SET shares that will help expedite the reconciliation and remittances at the same time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has created a simple program thru MS Excel wherein the Municipal Treasurer can generate real time schedule of RPT and SET shares.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality discontinue granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than their official traveling expenses and strictly adhere to the rules and regulations as required under Section 339 of RA No. 7160 and Items 4.1.4 and 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality discontinued granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than for their official traveling expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to continually coordinate with the DILG and other agencies involved in CBMS as provided in the IRR of RA 11315 to expedite the completion of CBMS that will establish/create an updated GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MPDC is in continuous coordination with the DILG regarding the status of CBMS. The OIC-MPDC has already collected updated data for each household and a planning and development database is now existing. The information relating to gender and development is now being used by the MGFPS to assist in the gender-responsive planning.
observations.reason: The CBMS was discontinued in CY 2019; hence completion remained incomplete as of December 31, 2020 despite coordination with the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by requiring the Municipal Engineer to submit the building plans and other officers concerned responsible for other documents in order to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality. Also, designate an administrative staff to assist the Municipal Treasurer in her work as ICO-GSO in connection with the insurance of properties.
observations.managementAction: The insurance application forms together with the supporting documents were already submitted to GSIS for their review and/or further processing. However, the GSIS noted some deficiencies in the attached documents, thus the concerned offices were directed to coordinate and submit the lacking requirements the soonest possible time. An administrative staff was already designated to assist the Municipal Treasurer in the insurance coverage of the Municipality’s properties and building plans were submitted by the Municipal Engineer on March 19, 2021. The remaining building plan for the newly constructed Post Harvest and Processing Facility is yet to be submitted.
observations.reason: COVID-19 pandemic and community quarantine restrictions impeded the processing of the GSIS Insurance application and some documentary deficiencies were noted by GSIS requiring completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to: follow-up from the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan 2016-2025; coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to achieve the Municipality’s sanitary landfill full operation; and continue to monitor the establishment and operation of MRF in the two remaining Barangays; and the Municipal Engineer to submit the Program of Works to the Municipal Budget Office so that any deficiency in the amount allocated for the construction of Material Recovery Facility will be included in the supplemental budget in accordance with implementing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO was advised to make a follow-up inquiry from NSWMC regarding the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan 2016-2025. The plan to fully operate the sanitary landfill is included in the priority programs of the Municipality. A Program of Works (POW) was already prepared for the establishment of MRF as an integral component of the proposed sanitary landfill. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) commenced with the public bidding process intended to be completed in the third quarter of CY 2021. Establishment and/or maintenance of MRF by every barangay will be closely monitored.
observations.reason: The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan 2016-2025 has not yet been approved by the NSWMC; COVID-19 pandemic and typhoons hindered full operation/construction; and two barangays remain to establish their own MRFs.

observations.recommendation: Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was unable to initiate the creation of permanent position for General Services Officer or any equivalent or similar position will be considered in the CY 2021 Annual Budget. While waiting for its creation, the designated ICO-GSO and the Municipal Accountant shall undertake the reconciliation of their records/schedules of the PPE.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert utmost effort to locate the documents to support the unaccounted balance of P496,032.38 in accordance with the provisions of Section 59 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has exerted its utmost effort in locating records relating the unaccounted balance of the Other Payable account. However, since the transactions occurred almost 10 years ago without subsidiary ledger and records or any schedule aside from the General Ledger balance, the assigned accounting personnel encountered difficulties in verifying the unaccounted balance. Considering that no person/party files any claim from this Municipality for such a long period of time, the account may be reverted as an addition to the Government Equity subject to the approval of the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: The assigned accounting personnel encountered difficulties in verifying the unaccounted balance due to the absence of subsidiary ledgers and records and lack of proper turnover from the previous Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the In-Charge of Office (ICO) - General Services Officer (GSO) to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by requiring both the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-GSO to expedite the completion of necessary documents required by the GSIS to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an administrative staff to assist the Municipal Treasurer in her work as ICO-GSO in connection with the insurance of properties.
observations.managementAction: The application together with the requirements for insurable properties are now being prepared in coordination with the Engineering Office and Accounting Office including the additional and/or substitute requirement which is the Certification from the Municipal Accountant on the Net Book Value of the insurable properties. However, since the Province of Quezon is under Modified General Community Quarantine, the movement of personnel is still regulated and the process of securing insurance covering the Municipality’s properties takes considerable length of time.
observations.reason: Completion of necessary documents is still in process and movement restrictions due to Modified General Community Quarantine delayed the process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (MGFPS) Focal Person to coordinate with the DILG to expedite the completion of CBMS that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MGAD Focal Person has already coordinated with the assigned MLGOO regarding the completion and/or finalization of the CBMS. In view that the survey was conducted two years ago and the data became outdated, the Municipality decided to abandon the CBMS and chose to adopt its own GAD Database in accordance with DILG Provincial Memorandum No. 2019-11. The prescribed forms have been accomplished and collected, and the development of GAD Database is now being prepared. For the meantime, the Municipality is using the sex-disaggregated data from the MSWDO and KALAHI CIDSS as its GAD Database in planning and implementation of various gender responsive programs.
observations.reason: CBMS was discontinued by DILG; survey data outdated; Municipality abandoned CBMS and is developing its own GAD Database which is not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor initiate the creation
of permanent position for General Services Officer with the concurrence of the
Sangguniang Bayan and create a Committee to undertake immediate disposal
of the unserviceable properties, to avoid further deterioration and possible loss
and to generate additional income from its disposal. Thereafter, require the
completion of the reconciliation of the property, plant and equipment account
in order to establish correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any
discrepancy that will be noted in accordance with the requirement of Paragraph
27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor, the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to continue
exerting extra effort to facilitate the immediate titling of the four parcels of lot
valued at P301,100.00 in the name of the Municipality and to determine/identify
the unidentified municipal lots worth P5,801,641.81 to avoid possible third party
claims that may arise in the future and for the fair presentation of the Land
account in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to complete and update the supplies ledger cards
to support the balances of all inventory accounts of the Municipality;
b. the ICO-GSO to maintain and update stock cards for the receipts and
issuances of supplies to support the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI); and
c. the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-GSO to coordinate for the
reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments, if any,
for the fair presentation of the inventory account in the financial statements
pursuant to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to exert utmost effort to locate the documents to support the
unaccounted balance of P507,923.36 in accordance with the provisions of
Section 59 of PD No. 1445 and with the requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest
expenses and consequently adjust the computation and recording of the
assets’ depreciation expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to coordinate with the previous Municipal Accountant in retrieving the
records/schedules of these funds for verification. Also, to confirm with the
source agencies any unliquidated balances due them. After which, require the
Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the source agencies the
unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds or to the National Treasury
for unidentified source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to promptly remit the RPT and SET share due to the
Provincial Government and the component Barangays within the prescribed
period in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section
271(d) of RA No. 7160 by reconciling and preparing the schedule of RPT and Set
shares on a timely basis.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with
the Municipal Treasurer and their staff, create a simple program thru MS Excel
that will generate real time schedule of RPT and SET share which will help
expedite the reconciliation and remittances at the same time.

We recommended that the Municipality discontinue granting cash advances to
elected officials for purposes other than their official traveling expenses to
adhere strictly to the rules and regulations as required under Section 339 of RA
No. 7160 and Items 4.1.4 and 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that require those responsible of the titling of the parcels of land as follows:
a. The Geodetic Engineer to collate the technical description of all untitled parcels of land and conduct the subdivision and prepare and/or the technical description.
b. The Municipal Assessor to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the various lots and/or deed of conveyance to facilitate ownership of the Municipality over the portion of the undivided land.
c. The Municipal Accountant to review and verify the CTCs from the Municipal Assessor for the preparation of subsidiary ledgers. Henceforth, require the coordination among the Geodetic Engineer, the Municipal Assessor, and the Municipal Accountant in the identifying the lots already titled and not, and those that are already booked in the municipal assets in order that the existence, valuation and ownership of land appearing in the financial statements are established.
observations.managementAction: The Geodetic Engineer started conducting the survey, collating the technical description and already prepared/submitted some technical description need for the eventual titling of the other parcels of land. The Municipal Assessor exerted effort to identify the mother title and the location of the lots acquired by the previous administration and retrieved available records and/or deed of conveyance. The Municipal Accountant reviewed and verified the unaccounted OCT/TCTs and updated the subsidiary ledger of the Land account.
observations.reason: The Geodetic Engineer is still in the process of identifying and verifying the details of unidentified lots. Henceforth, coordination among the Geodetic Engineer, the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Accountant in the identifying the lots already titled and not, and those that are already booked in the municipal assets is still ongoing. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: Coordinate with the Accounting Office to properly recognize the donations received from the DOH. Henceforth, ensure that donations receive from other agencies are properly recognized.
observations.managementAction: All donated drugs and medicines were properly recorded and accounted by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to facilitate the conduct of study needed in the operation of the sanitary landfill and coordinate with the concerned National Government Agencies for its immediate approval. Further, monitor the establishment and/or operation of MRF in the Barangays. Consider hiring additional personnel to supervise the operation in the open dumpsite subject to availability of funds.
observations.managementAction: The development of sanitary landfill is already on-going and the P1,000,000.00 budget is being utilized in the construction of access roads to-and-from the proposed site. The MENRO has secured the needed approval from the Mines and Geosciences Bureau. However, its full development is being deferred due to the financial capability of the Municipality. The Municipal Mayor has issued a Memorandum requiring the Punong Barangay to establish its own MRF and to allocate funds thereof. Moreover, the proper segregation of solid waste was given force and effect through the issuance of a reiterating Memorandum.
observations.reason: Full development of sanitary landfill deferred due to financial capability of the Municipality; remaining barangays have yet to establish MRFs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the previous Municipal Accountant in retrieving the records/schedules of these funds for verification. Also, to confirm with the source agencies any unliquidated balances due them. After which, require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the source agencies the unexpended/unutilized balances of the trust funds or to the National Treasury for unidentified source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given to the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant. She was advised to deposit the checks representing the remittances of the unutilized balances of the Mangrove Rehabilitation Project, DOST-CEST and DOH-Sea Ambulance. The Accounting Office has conducted the review of old records/cashbooks in order to verify transactions involving KKK Fund, DA-Sale of Rice, SOLCENTAF, Funds for certified seeds, Office of the President and Bureau of Soils-SSIP. With regard to the remaining balance of MLGP, additional funds were transferred to the Municipality this CY 2020 for the continuation of the program.
observations.reason: Previous Municipal Accountant did not turn over records/schedules; review and verification required; some remittances done and review ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC-MDRRMO and the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of LDRRM Fund through proper planning and to scrutinize the planned PPAs for disaster management in order to efficiently utilize and implement the LDRRM Fund. (Note: recommendation text in table references hiring technical personnel capable of using AutoCAD, coordinate with winning bidder for submission of CSHP and prepare DED/CSHP).
observations.managementAction: The PPAs indicated in the 2019 Annual Municipal Investment Program and Annual Investment Program under 20% Development Fund were prepared and the rules and regulations regarding CHSP were faithfully complied with. Most of the DED projects under 20% Development Fund were prepared and the necessary documents were completed.
observations.reason: The Municipality prepared and completed DED projects and complied with CHSP regulations; personnel recruitment for Engineer II was approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to promptly remit the RPT and SET share due to the Provincial Government and the component Barangays within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 by reconciling and preparing the schedule of RPT and SET shares on a timely basis. Further, recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and their staff, create a simple program thru MS Excel that will generate real time schedule of RPT and SET share which will help expedite the reconciliation and remittances at the same time.
observations.managementAction: The barangay and provincial shares on RPT and SET are now being remitted within the prescribed period. In addition, the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office has developed a simple excel program that will generate proper amount of remittances to expedite the reconciliation.
observations.reason: Initial delays were due to voluminous transactions around statutory deadlines and limitations of prior MS Excel reconciliation tools; system improvements have been made and remittances are now being done within prescribed periods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in ensuring that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided in Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018. Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in the GAA is strictly observed yearly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum monthly net take home pay provided in the GAA is strictly observed yearly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC-MDRRMO and the OIC-MPDC to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF through proper planning and to scrutinize the planned PPAs for disaster management in order to efficiently utilize and implement the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Most of the PPAs indicated in the 2019 Municipal Investment Program and Annual Investment Program under 70% Mitigation Fund of LDRRMF were prudently utilized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the ICO–General Services Officer to reconcile the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the documents to support the unaccounted balance of P497,582.38 pursuant to the provisions of Section 59 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 4, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel; inability to locate prior records/files.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO – General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that the required documents for the insurance of the insurable properties of the Municipality are now being prepared in coordination with the Engineering Office because the GSIS Insurance Application Form requires technical specifications which could not be determined by mere copies of pertinent documents attached to the disbursement voucher especially so with the application of insurance buildings which requires details for materials used in the construction of the entire building.
observations.reason: Absence of documents required by the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury the amount of P285,980.17 representing unexpended/unutilized balances of trust funds.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 7, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to retrieve/locate files and records of funds received in prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC- Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) and the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that the planned PPAs for disaster risk management are fully implemented on target implementation period to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to natural and man-made hazards/disasters.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 11, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: Due to poor planning and delayed implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the CBMS that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 12, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: Due to non-completion of the CBMS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the creation of permanent
position for GSO with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan and create a
Committee to undertake immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, to
avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income
from its disposal. Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the
property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE
balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require those responsible of the titling
of the parcels of land as follows:
a. The Geodetic Engineer to collate the technical description of all untitled
parcels of land and conduct the subdivision survey.
b. The Municipal Assessor to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly
issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of
the various lots and cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the
Municipality and to exert extra effort to identify/locate those lots that were
acquired by previous administration.
c. The Municipal Accountant to review and verify the CTCs from the Municipal
Assessor for the preparation of subsidiary ledgers.
Henceforth, require the coordination among the Geodetic Engineer, the Municipal
Assessor, and the Municipal Accountant in identifying the lots already titled and
not, and those that are already booked in the municipal assets in order that the
existence, valuation and ownership of land appearing in the financial statements
are established.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MHO to coordinate with the
Accounting Office to properly recognize the donations received from the DOH.
Henceforth, ensure that donations receive from other agencies are properly
recognized.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert
extra effort to locate the documents to support the unaccounted balance of
P496,032.38 pursuant to the provisions of Section 59 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the In-Charge of Office (ICO) –
General Services Officer (GSO) to ensure that all the insurable properties of the
Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with
the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by requiring both the
Municipal Accountant and the ICO-GSO to provide the necessary supporting
documents required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for
property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 advise the remaining barangays to establish their own MRF pursuant to
Section 32, Article 4 of RA 9003;
 provide additional budget to develop and operate a fully functional sanitary
landfill including machineries and equipment, as a final disposal site for solid
and, eventually, residual wastes of the municipality; and
 consider hiring additional personnel to supervise the operation in the open
dumpsite subject to availability of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prioritize the retrieval of files/records of these funds for verification. After which,
require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury
the amount of P282,119.17 representing unexpended/unutilized balances of trust
funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider hiring additional draftsman
capable of using AutoCAD software to avoid delays subject to availability of funds
and CSC rules and regulations and to coordinate with the winning bidder to cause
the immediate submission of CSHP for timely issuance of NTP so that all the
programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and
completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the
maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to promptly remit the RPT and SET share due the Provincial
Government and the component Barangays within the prescribed period in
accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No.
7160 by reconciling and preparing the schedule of RPT and SET shares on a timely
basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal HRMO to
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in ensuring that an employee’s net take
home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum allowed take home pay of
P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018.
Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in the
GAA is strictly observed yearly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC – MDRRMO and the OIC –
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the
utilization of LDRRM Fund through proper planning and to scrutinize the planned
PPAs for disaster risk management in order to efficiently utilize and implement the
LDRRM fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the various lots and cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality and to exert extra effort to identify/locate those lots that were acquired by previous administration. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in identifying the lots booked in the municipal assets in order to maintain the appropriate Subsidiary Ledger containing the details and breakdown of the Land account.
observations.managementAction: Gathered documents and identified the land own by the Municipality is still in the process of collating the technical description of all untitled properties of the Municipality. Of the total P6,402,741.81 balance of Land account, only P601,100.00 was with appropriate subsidiary ledger. Also, the presented transfer certificate titles are still for review and verification by the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of subsidiary ledgers. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report
observations.reason:
1. The hired Geodetic Engineer who will conduct the subdivision survey is still in the process of collating the technical description of all untitled properties of the Municipality.
2. Of the total P6,402,741.81 balance of Land account, only P601,100.00 was with appropriate subsidiary ledger.
3. The presented transfer certificate titles are still for review and verification by the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. ICO – General Services Officer (GSO) to:  conduct the physical count of all inventories of the Municipality;  prepare and submit immediately the inventory reports;  maintain complete stock cards and submit the required RPCI to the Audit Team; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC); c. ICO-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records regularly; and d. Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Conducted the physical count and submitted the required RPCI to the Audit Team. Stock cards were already prepared and submitted. Supplies Ledger Cards are now being maintained.
observations.reason: Conducted the physical count and submitted the required RPCI to the Audit Team; Stock cards were already prepared and submitted; Supplies Ledger Cards are now being maintained. However, recognition of donated drugs and medicines remains an issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to integrate the Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality and require the contractors to submit Construction Safety and Health Program duly-approved by DOLE upon signing of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: The Contractors are being required to submit their CSHP duly-approved by the DOLE. The Municipal Engineer integrated the Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction cost of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The cost of CSHP is being integrated as separate line item in the computation of construction cost of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality. Contractors are being required to submit CSHP duly-approved by DOLE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to establish their own MRF;  seek an ordinance from the Municipal Sangguniang Bayan to strictly implement the waste segregation at source and the no segregation/no collection rule;  designate a personnel to supervise the operation in the open dumpsite; and  provide additional budget to develop and operate a fully functional sanitary landfill including machineries and equipment, as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of the municipality after the safe closure of the current open dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: Acquired an acre of land ideal for the development of sanitary landfill. Issued Ordinance No. 009-2018 “No Segregation, No Collection Rule”. Additional six barangays have established their own MRF from only one in CY 2017. Planning to hire personnel to supervise the operation of the dumpsite and eventually the sanitary landfill after its development.
observations.reason: Lack of funds; Lack of personnel. For implementation in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to regularly review and compare her cashbook entries for both cash in local treasury and cash in bank accounts with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items, if any, to ensure reconciliation and reliability of the cashbook balances.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the cashbook entries with the records of the accounting is now being made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC – Municipal Planning and Development Council to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Planning and Development Council implemented the prior year’s development projects in 2018 and took steps to address delays. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 8, Part II of this Report
observations.reason: The 20% Development Fund prior year’s development was not optimally utilized due to lack of technical personnel and delayed submission of CSHP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the ICO–General Services Officer to reconcile the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to look into the Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the various lots and cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality and to exert extra effort to identify/locate those lots that were acquired by previous administration.

Also, require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in identifying the lots booked in the municipal assets in order to maintain the appropriate Subsidiary Ledger containing the details and breakdown of the Land account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
ICO – General Services Officer (GSO) to:
conduct the physical count of all inventories of the Municipality;
prepare and submit immediately the inventory reports; and
maintain complete stock cards
Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC);
ICO-GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records regularly; and
Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the documents to support the unaccounted balance of P497,582.38 pursuant to the provisions of Section 59 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO – GeneralServices Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RANo. 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to integrate the Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality and require the contractors to submit Construction Safety and Health Program duly-approved by DOLE upon signing of the contracts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury the amount of P285,980.17 representing unexpended/unutilized balances of trust funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to regularly review and compare her cashbook entries for both cash in local treasury and cash in bank accounts with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items, if any, to ensure reconciliation and reliability of the cashbook balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC – Municipal Planning and Development Council to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC-Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) and the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that the planned PPAs for disaster risk management are fully implemented on target implementation period to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to natural and man-made hazards/disasters.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayorinitiate the establishment of a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory and prepare and submit the RPCPPE per type of property. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant and ICO-General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding ledger cards and property cards for all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-General Services Officer submitted the RPCPPE per type of property to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCPPE were not reconciled with the accounting records. Reiterated under observation no. 1, Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to look into Transfer Certificate of Title duly issued by the Registry of Deeds, if any and/or the supporting tax declaration of the various lots and cause the immediate titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality.

Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledger for the Land account.
observations.managementAction:
Hired the service of Geodetic Engineer to conduct subdivision survey of municipal lots. Various lots were already supported with Transfer Certificate Title in the name of the Municipality.

Prepared partial subsidiary ledger.
observations.reason:
Survey is yet to be conducted since the data for Municipal lots are not yet completed.

Not prioritized. 
Reiterated under observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury the amount of P1,177,568.81 representing unexpended/unutilized balances of trust funds and PDAF.
observations.managementAction: Remitted the balance of PDAF to the National Treasury. Reclassify the balances recorded under Due to LGUs.
observations.reason: Reiterated under observation no. 8, Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holder of checks issued one (1) month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books of the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered particularly during the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer is monitoring her check issuances, sending notices to payeeswhose checks are going to become stale and posting available checks for release at her office.

Necessary adjusting entries were made on the bank reconciliation every time staled check was discovered.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The reports were submitted beyond the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the former MDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year. Likewise, submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the report on the list of all on-going government projects/programs/ activities (PPA).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer complied with stamping “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to ensure that near maturing obligations are handled with utmost care to avoid the unnecessary penalties and to refund the amount of P10,683.61 for the penalty of late payments. Payment of electricity and other basic utilities shall be given priority and shall be made on or before its due date.
observations.managementAction: The whole penalty was deducted on the succeeding billing statements after the exit conference on May 8, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit APP-CSE to DBM as required under Circular Letter No. 2011-06 and 2011-06A, and to submit to the Audit Team copy of approved APP indicating the mode of procurement to be adopted on all its procurement consistent with the duly approved yearly budget.

We also recommended the BAC Chairman to provide the Audit Team copy of BAC resolutions/recommendations on the mode of procurement adopted in all the transactions listed in the Annex A for further verification of the Team.
observations.managementAction:
Emailed to DBM outside the deadline set.

Submitted the copy of BAC resolutions/ recommendations on the mode of procurement adopted in all the transaction to the Audit Team
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer submit to the Auditor the request for exemption with justification duly approved by the NCPAM Program Director, if any, for the drugs and medicines not included in the latest PNF Manual as stated above, otherwise disallowed in audit.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Officer to procure only those drugs and medicines identified in the PNF Manual and issue certification that products being requisitioned and procured fall within and conform with the latest edition of the PNF Manual. Moreover, direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to ensure that all purchases of drugs and medicines are supported with the said certification by the requisitioning officer or by the duly authorized officer.
observations.managementAction:
Submitted the justification.


The drugs and medicine purchases are now based on the latest PNF Manual.


Certification is now being attached to the Purchase Request.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Mayor to exert more effort in obtaining copies of the Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement on all funds received from the National Government Agencies and furnish the same to the Audit Team, as provided for in Item 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement and/or liquidations are supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
Provided copies of MOA to the Audit Team.



Disbursements were supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point to regularly submit the Municipality’s approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adhere to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the affected accounts and remitted the remaining balances of to the concerned agencies.
After two failed bidding, the project for Subdivision Survey for the remaining untitled real properties of the Municipality is now under Negotiated Procurement.

Reiterated under Observation No. 7, Part II of this report.
